note: mock_rate encodes the IDEAL outcome so `--decider mock` demonstrates the pipeline; real deciders (api/claude-cli) ignore it and measure the description. sec-scan is the comprehensive OWASP Top 10:2025 audit that orchestrates the specialized scans — it owns broad, whole-codebase / pre-prod reviews. Negatives are near-misses scoped narrowly enough that a specialist sibling wins (sec-secrets, sec-deps, sec-iac, sec-gate, sec-ci, sec-compliance, sec-threat-model, sec-pentest, sec-fix) or a plain debug/explain action.

queries.text: run a full security scan on the whole codebase before we ship to prod
can you do an OWASP Top 10 audit of this service? we're going through due diligence
comprehensive security review of the payments API — injection, authz, crypto, SSRF, the works
my manager asked for a pre-prod security review of the backend before Friday's release
harden this service — do a deep security audit across secrets, deps, and the code itself
we're being acquired and their team wants a security audit of the repo, can you run one end to end
full sec audit of src/ please — I want deserialization, SSRF and broken access control all covered
do a thorough security pass over the entire app and give me a prioritized findings report
marvin security scan the codebase — we haven't audited this in over a year
before the launch I want the big security audit: run all the scanners plus a manual review for logic bugs and crypto misuse
scan the whole git history for leaked API keys and tokens we may have committed
queries.note: secret detection across code+history → sec-secrets, not the full audit

queries.text: audit package.json and package-lock for known CVEs and typosquatted packages
review our Terraform and Kubernetes manifests for over-permissioned IAM and exposed endpoints
quick security check on my staged diff before I commit
audit our GitHub Actions workflows for supply chain risk and secret exposure
check this code against OWASP ASVS L2 and give me a control-by-control compliance matrix
generate a STRIDE threat model for the new file-upload feature
here's a Semgrep finding for a SQL injection — write and verify a patch for it
put together a penetration testing checklist for our auth and API surface
explain how our authentication middleware validates session tokens
queries.note: understanding code behavior → explain, not a security audit

queries.text: full security audit of the service before release — I especially care about the dependencies and any leaked secrets
queries.note: names deps + secrets but the ask is a comprehensive whole-codebase audit that orchestrates them → must beat sec-deps

queries.text: we're prepping for a SOC 2 audit — do a broad security review of the entire codebase and flag everything risky
queries.note: 'audit' + compliance context, but the request is a broad codebase-wide scan, not an ASVS control matrix → must beat sec-compliance

queries.text: just scan the repo and git history for committed credentials and private keys
queries.note: scoped strictly to leaked-secret detection across code+history → sec-secrets wins over the broad scan

queries.text: quick secure-this-diff gate on my staged changes before I push the branch
queries.note: diff-scoped fast pre-commit check, not a full codebase audit → sec-gate wins
